pages.text: The Storage Core is the heart of one network. It owns the stored items and shared energy, while attached Units provide type capacity and Terminals provide access.

The same Core item keeps its contents through saves and every supported break path. A packed Core uses a one-time server recovery token; place that exact item to restore its inventory, energy, machines, and identity.

If your still-unplaced packed Core item is lost, run $(thing)/magic_storage recover_core$() to reissue your latest unclaimed Core. A duplicate token cannot duplicate contents. Data lost before this recovery system existed cannot be reconstructed.

Removing Storage Units never deletes items already held by the Core, but it can leave too little room for new types. Before moving a busy network, pick up the data-carrying Core safely and make sure you can identify it among blank Cores.
See $(l:magic_storage:recipes)Recipe Catalog$() for the exact crafting layout.
